title: Mattermost vs Microsoft Teams for Power users
meta_description: See why Microsoft Teams fails first when the system requires vendor-hosted infrastructure instead of allowing self-hosted deployment and why Mattermost is the better pick for power users.
constraint_lens: You need a collaboration tool that gives you full control over where and how it is deployed.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for power users who need self-hosted deployment and full infrastructure control.
one_second_verdict.reason: Microsoft Teams fails first because it relies on vendor-hosted infrastructure instead of allowing self-hosting.

verdict.summary: Mattermost is the better choice when you need full control over your deployment environment. It supports self-hosted setups, allowing you to run the system within restricted or private infrastructure. Microsoft Teams relies on vendor-hosted SaaS infrastructure, which limits control and may not meet requirements for secure or isolated environments.
verdict.decision_rule: If the system requires vendor-hosted infrastructure instead of allowing self-hosted deployment, Microsoft Teams fails first.

sections.heading: Why Mattermost fits this situation
sections.content: This setup fits a power user operating in a restricted environment where external SaaS platforms are not an option. Without self-hosting, the system cannot be used at all. Mattermost enables full control over deployment and infrastructure.

bullets.point: Supports self-hosted deployment within private infrastructure.
bullets.why_it_matters: You retain full control over data and environment.

bullets.point: Works in restricted or isolated environments without relying on external vendors.
bullets.why_it_matters: This ensures compliance with security or operational requirements.

bullets.point: Allows customization and control at the infrastructure level.
bullets.why_it_matters: This provides flexibility for advanced use cases.

bullets.point: Provides a fully managed SaaS experience with no infrastructure to maintain.
bullets.why_it_matters: This reduces setup effort, but removes deployment control.

bullets.point: Handles hosting, updates, and scaling automatically.
bullets.why_it_matters: This simplifies usage, but limits customization and control.
This makes it unsuitable for restricted or self-hosted requirements.

sections.heading: How each tool can break down

items.fails_when: Mattermost starts to break when you do not have the resources or need to manage your own infrastructure.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Microsoft Teams if you prefer a fully managed SaaS solution.

items.fails_when: Microsoft Teams starts to break when you cannot use vendor-hosted infrastructure due to security or deployment constraints.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Mattermost when self-hosting is required.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: This verdict might flip if you do not need control over deployment and prefer a fully managed cloud solution. In that case, Microsoft Teams may be more suitable.

sections.heading: Quick decision rules
rules: Pick Mattermost if you need self-hosted deployment.
Pick Microsoft Teams if you prefer a managed SaaS solution.
If infrastructure control matters, choose Mattermost.

faqs.q: Why does Mattermost win for power users?
faqs.a: Because it allows self-hosted deployment and full control over infrastructure.
No, it relies on vendor-hosted SaaS infrastructure.

faqs.q: When should I choose Microsoft Teams instead?
faqs.a: Choose it when you prefer a fully managed cloud solution without handling infrastructure.

faqs.q: What is the main difference between these tools?
faqs.a: Mattermost supports self-hosting, while Microsoft Teams relies on vendor-hosted infrastructure.
